Task 3 · Product

Digital Portfolio: English Singing Competition

A collection of texts written around one campus event — from the formal request that made it possible to the evaluative report that closed it out.

NameLoh Boon Chong
ProgrammePPISMP 2025/2030 BC SJKC
InstituteInstitut Pendidikan Guru Kampus Batu Lintang
Official Event Poster
Text 01 — Formal Letter

Formal Letter: Request Permission

Every successful competition begins with official approval. This formal letter is addressed to the Director of IPG Kampus Batu Lintang and follows the standard conventions of formal letter writing to respectfully seek official approval to organise the "Voice of Dreams" English Singing Competition on campus.

Page 1
Page 2
Text 02 — Informal Correspondence

Informal Correspondence: Inviting a Friend

After receiving approval for the event, the next text is an informal email to my close friend, Jack, from another IPG campus. It invites him to attend the singing competition and highlights the exciting activities and attractions of the event.

Email
Text 03 — Online Post

Online Post: Sharing The Moment

The morning after, a reflective post goes up on the ELS Instagram — part recap, part personal diary entry, capturing what the competition actually felt like from behind the scenes.

ELS
loh.bc
TAZA, IPGKBL
♡ 💬 ↗
loh.bc 🎶✨ A Night to Remember: "Voice Of Dreams" EngLish Singing Competition 2026 ✨🎤

What an incredible journey it has been! After weeks of preparation, countless rehearsals, and endless teamwork, the English Language Singing Competition has finally come to an unforgettable end.

As the chairperson of this event, I felt a mixture of excitement, nervousness, and pride throughout the entire process. Standing behind the stage and watching each participant give their best performance reminded me that this competition was not only about winning a prize, but also about building confidence, expressing creativity, and creating meaningful memories.

Seeing our participants step out of their comfort zones and perform in front of a crowd was truly inspiring. Every voice carried a unique story, and every performance showed the courage and passion they had developed along the way.

I would like to express my deepest appreciation to my amazing committee members for their dedication and hard work, our lecturers for their guidance and support, and everyone who contributed to making this event a success.

Voice of IPG 2026 was more than just a competition — it was a celebration of talent, confidence, and friendship. Thank you to everyone who joined us and made this event a memorable chapter. 💛🎶
#VoiceOfDreams2026 #EnglishSingingCompetition #IPGKBL #CelebrateTalent
Text 04 — Evaluative Report

From Notes to Final Report

This section documents the complete development process of the event evaluation report, illustrating how the initial draft was systematically refined into a polished final version. As the evaluation report carries the greatest significance within this portfolio, every stage of the writing process—from preliminary notes and the first draft to editing, revision, editing and the final report—has been carefully recorded. Use the tabs below to follow each stage of the report's development in sequence.

Pre-Writing (Brainstorming & Outlining)

Before initiating the report draft, an Idea Funnel approach was utilized to analyze the specific requirements of the evaluation task, recognizing that a professional report requires non-personal subjects, passive structures, and precise past-tense records of findings.

Brainstorm & Outline

Drafting (Initial Text Structure)

The initial layout structure focused exclusively on constructing the raw analytical framework without excessive stylistic refinement.

Documented Initial Draft

Revision (Peer Feedback Loop)

In accordance with the revision workflow, Draft Version 1 was subjected to critical evaluation by two peers (acting as the Architectural Reviewers) to identify structural, stylistic, and tone deficiencies before any micro-editing took place.

Peer Review & Annotation

Editing (Micro-Level Refinement)

The editing stage acted as the micro-level proofreader, focusing strictly on sentence mechanics, proper formatting, past tense consistency for findings, and ensuring no personal pronouns survived the revision process. The layout was adjusted to incorporate precise corporate text formatting.

Editing Proofsheet Checkpoints

Publishing (Final Polished Critical Report)

The following is the clean, publication-ready critical review report, fully optimized according to formal structural and academic criteria.

Final Report
